title: "I hate Xrandr!"

### Post by whoopn on 2008-06-16
Ok why did we go and remove the only video configuration program that has ever worked for linux from 7.04?

I have hardy heron and xrandr (which was working before I added some memory to my computer) all of the sudden doesn't want to work with my monitor and video card correctly.

Ok the issue:
I want to force 1280x1024 at 60hz but xrandr wont let me in the gui.

Here is my xorg.conf

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Mitsubishi"
	Displaysize	304 228
	Modeline "1280x1024_60.00"  108.88  1280 1360 1496 1712  1024 1025 1028 1060  -HSync +Vsync
    	Option "PreferredMode" "1280x1024_60.00"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
EndSection
```

Please help...I'm frustrated to the point of either reverting back 7.04 or installing windows...at least the display configurator always works in windows...

---

### Post by whoopn on 2008-06-17
Scratch that!

I had to change the "Screen" section to use the Monitor I had defined...haha, I feel foolish now...


Please change to [Solved] moderators!
